assumptions: No Notion account, workspace, workspace role, developer-portal access, personal access token, environment variable, private API-created page, or prior request is assumed.
A browser and terminal with cURL are available; an email, Google account, or Microsoft account is available for the current signup choices.
No account was created, provider was authorized, terms were accepted, workspace was created or joined, administrator setting was changed, token was created, or API request was sent during research.

developer_goal: Create a Notion account and workspace, generate a personal access token, send the API quickstart page-creation request, and open the resulting private page.

first_success.outcome: The intended terminal is a private page named `Hello from the API`, created from the published Markdown request and visible through the returned URL or the Private section of the Notion sidebar.
first_success.observable_signal: The API returns a page object with an ID and `url`; opening it shows the heading, paragraph, and bullet list, and the guide states that the first request has been made.

route_selection.surface: Notion API quickstart with a personal access token
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Notion API quickstart with a personal access token
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Internal connection developer quickstart
candidates.reason: The current API quickstart says a personal access token is faster for a user-owned script or CLI, while internal connections demonstrate team-owned automation and explicit page sharing.

candidates.name: Notion CLI and Workers quickstart
candidates.reason: This is a separate Workers product route that installs the CLI, deploys a worker, and runs a tool rather than making the API quickstart's one page-creation request.

candidates.name: Public connection with OAuth
candidates.reason: OAuth is the multi-workspace route, while the selected API quickstart uses a personal token for the developer's own workspace.

required_path.action: Complete Notion account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Notion account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Notion candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The API returns a page object with an ID and `url`; opening it shows the heading, paragraph, and bullet list, and the guide states that the first request has been made.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Have a Notion account and workspace.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S3']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'Have permission to create a personal access token; Business and Enterprise workspaces restrict PAT creation by default and require a workspace owner to enable it.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'credential', 'requirement': 'Create, copy, and securely store a personal access token because it cannot be viewed again.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

external_gates.description: The API quickstart requires an existing Notion account and workspace.
The current signup offers work email, Google, or Microsoft.
Continuing signup acknowledges Notion's Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Create a page in the developer's Notion workspace with a personal access token and a single API request.

excluded.item: Creating databases, querying content, page sharing, internal connections, OAuth, public distribution, comments, files, Workers, webhooks, rate-limit handling, and production token management occur aft
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Overview - Notion Docs
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

sources.title: API quickstart - Notion Docs
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for API quickstart - Notion Docs

sources.title: Notion - Sign up
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Notion - Sign up

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Notion show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
